# Cinch

> Remote clipboard for developers. Push from any terminal, pull on any machine.

Cinch is an open-source CLI tool that pipes remote terminal output straight to your local clipboard. It uses a self-hostable WebSocket relay with SQLite persistence for reliable, real-time clipboard delivery across machines.

## Core Commands

- `cinch push` — Pipe any command output to the relay (e.g., `cat file.txt | cinch push`)
- `cinch pull` — Pull the latest clip to stdout or your local clipboard
- `cinch auth` — Pair devices, check status, or logout

## Key Features

- Unix-native: composable with pipes, works with any command
- WebSocket relay: real-time delivery with SQLite persistence
- Self-hostable: run your own relay server via Docker or binary
- Desktop agent: automatic clipboard sync on macOS
- MIT licensed, fully open source

## Links

- Homepage: https://cinch.jinmu.me
- Documentation: https://cinch.jinmu.me/docs/
- Getting Started: https://cinch.jinmu.me/docs/getting-started/
- CLI Reference (push): https://cinch.jinmu.me/docs/cli/push/
- CLI Reference (pull): https://cinch.jinmu.me/docs/cli/pull/
- CLI Reference (auth): https://cinch.jinmu.me/docs/cli/auth/
- Self-hosting Guide: https://cinch.jinmu.me/docs/relay/self-hosting/
- Source Code: https://github.com/JinmuGo/cinch

## Installation

```bash
curl -fsSL cinch.jinmu.me/install.sh | sh
```
